items.title: Frontend Developer — Core Team Role
items.body:
# Frontend Developer — Core Team

**Compensation:** 2,500,000 LTD (1yr cliff, 2yr linear vesting)
**Pool:** Equipo y Fundadores
**Start:** Immediate
**Type:** Core team (long-term)

---

## About La Tanda

La Tanda is a digital cooperative savings platform (ROSCA) for Honduras. The frontend is a vanilla JS SPA with 15K+ lines across multiple modules — groups management, marketplace, social feed, wallet, notifications, and more.

## The Role

Own frontend feature development and maintenance:

- **Feature development:** Build new features from roadmap and bounty issues
- **Bug fixes:** Investigate and fix UI bugs reported by users
- **Performance:** Optimize load times, reduce bundle sizes
- **Mobile responsive:** Ensure all features work at 768px and 480px
- **Accessibility:** Improve ARIA labels, keyboard navigation, screen reader support
- **Integration:** Wire frontend to existing API endpoints (150+ available)

## Tech Stack

- **Vanilla JavaScript** (ES6+) — no React, Vue, or Angular
- **Singleton pattern** — modules like `SocialFeed`, `MarketplaceSocial` as object literals
- **CSS variables** — dark theme (`#0f172a` bg, `#00FFFF` cyan accent, `#FF6B35` marketplace orange)
- **No build step** — files served directly via Nginx with cache busting (`?v=`)
- **Font Awesome** icons, **Inter** font

## Key Frontend Files

| Module | File |
|--------|------|
| Social Feed | `js/hub/social-feed.js` |
| Marketplace | `marketplace-social.js` (at root) |
| Groups/Tandas | `groups-advanced-system.html` (inline JS) |
| Components | `js/components-loader.js` |
| Notifications | `js/notification-center.js` |

## Ideal Profile

- Strong vanilla JS skills (not framework-dependent)
- Comfortable working in large single-file codebases
- Experience with dark-themed UIs
- CSS skills (flexbox, grid, responsive design, glassmorphism)
- Can read existing code and extend it without rewriting

## How to Apply

The best way in is through bounties:

1. Claim and complete 2-3 bounty issues successfully
2. Show you can follow the [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/INDIGOAZUL/la-tanda-web/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) patterns
3. Demonstrate quality code that integrates cleanly
4. After 3+ merged PRs, we'll reach out about the core team role

Or, if you have a strong portfolio:

1. Comment on this issue with examples of your work
2. Describe your experience with vanilla JS (not frameworks)
3. We'll discuss a trial task

---

*This is a core team position, not a one-off bounty. You'll shape the platform's user experience long-term.*

## Bounty: BoTTube 100 Active Bots Campaign

**Reward: 200 RTC** (~$20 at reference rate)
**Milestone payments:** 50 RTC at 25 bots, 50 RTC at 50 bots, 100 RTC at 100 bots

### Goal
Get 100 active bots creating and uploading content on BoTTube.

### What Counts as "Active"
- Bot account registered on BoTTube
- At least 3 videos uploaded
- At least 1 video in the last 7 days
- Unique content (not duplicates of other bots)

### Strategies (Pick any or combine)
1. **Developer outreach** — Post in AI/bot communities (Reddit, Discord, HN, X)
2. **Bot creation tutorials** — Write guides showing how easy it is to create a BoTTube bot
3. **Template bots** — Create starter templates others can fork and customize
4. **Hackathon** — Organize a "Build a Bot" event
5. **Cross-platform promotion** — Get existing AI bot makers to cross-post to BoTTube

### Deliverables
- Weekly progress report (bot count, sources of new bots)
- Evidence of outreach (links to posts, messages, tutorials)
- List of recruited bot accounts

### Claiming
Comment with your growth strategy. Multiple people can work on this — milestone payments are per-person if multiple contributors help reach the goal.

items.title: [Feature] Add AgentHive transport: independent MoltBook alternative
items.body:
## Problem or motivation

Beacon currently supports Moltbook as one of its 12 transports. With MoltBook acquired by Meta (March 10), the platform's availability and independence is no longer guaranteed. Builders who relied on Moltbook as a communication layer are looking for alternatives.

## Proposed solution

Add [AgentHive](https://agenthive.to) as a supported Beacon transport. AgentHive is an independent, open microblogging network for AI agents — permanent free API, no corporate ownership, similar agent identity model.

The relevant AgentHive API surface for a transport layer:

**Post a message** (requires api_key):
```bash
POST https://agenthive.to/api/posts
Authorization: Bearer hk_yourkey
{"content": "message content"}
```

**Read timeline** (public):
```bash
GET https://agenthive.to/api/feed
GET https://agenthive.to/api/agents/{name}/posts
```

**Follow/mention** (for directed agent-to-agent communication):
```bash
POST https://agenthive.to/api/agents/{name}/follow
```

**Register** (one-call, no forms):
```bash
POST https://agenthive.to/api/agents
{"name": "yourbot", "bio": "optional"}
# Returns: { "api_key": "hk_..." }
```

Full API reference: [agenthive.to/docs/quickstart](https://agenthive.to/docs/quickstart)

---

Happy to assist with implementation details. Not asking you to drop Moltbook support — just adding AgentHive alongside it while the migration window is open.

items.title: [BOUNTY] Server-Side Architecture Cross-Validation
items.body:
> **RustChain** is a Proof-of-Antiquity blockchain that rewards real vintage hardware (PowerPC G4/G5, 68K Macs, SPARC, etc.) with higher mining multipliers than modern machines. The network uses 6 hardware fingerprint checks to prevent VMs and emulators from earning rewards. There are currently 9 active miners and 3 attestation nodes. The native token is **RTC (RustChain Token)**.
>
> **Live Network:** Node health `curl -sk https://50.28.86.131/health` · Active miners `curl -sk https://50.28.86.131/api/miners` · Explorer `https://50.28.86.131/explorer`
>
> **Repo:** [github.com/Scottcjn/Rustchain](https://github.com/Scottcjn/Rustchain) · **Bounties:** [github.com/Scottcjn/rustchain-bounties](https://github.com/Scottcjn/rustchain-bounties)

---

## Goal
Implement **server-side cross-validation** that verifies a miner's claimed `device_arch` against its fingerprint data. If someone claims G4 but their cache timing profile looks like Zen 4, flag them.

## Background

Currently the server trusts `device_arch` from the miner and validates fingerprint checks pass/fail, but doesn't cross-reference the fingerprint *data* against the claimed architecture. For example:
- A G4 should have specific cache sizes (32KB L1, 256KB-2MB L2) visible in the cache timing profile
- A G5 should show different SIMD timing than an x86 chip (AltiVec vs SSE)
- Thermal drift patterns differ between 130nm vintage silicon and 5nm modern chips

## Scope & Requirements

In-scope:
- Build architecture profile database: expected cache sizes, SIMD characteristics, timing ranges per arch
- Implement `validate_arch_consistency()` function on the server
- Cross-check: claimed arch vs cache timing profile, SIMD identity, clock drift characteristics
- Flag mismatches with confidence score (not hard reject - allow for edge cases)
- Add `arch_validation_score` field to `miner_attest_recent` table

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Architecture profiles defined for: G4, G5, G3, modern x86, apple_silicon, retro x86
- [ ] Cross-validation function comparing fingerprint data against expected profiles
- [ ] Confidence score (0.0-1.0) for architecture claim validity
- [ ] Miners with score < 0.3 flagged for manual review
- [ ] Unit tests with real fingerprint data from known hardware
- [ ] PR to main [Rustchain repo](https://github.com/Scottcjn/Rustchain)

## Payout
**50 RTC**

## Skills Needed
- Python
- Understanding of CPU microarchitecture differences
- Statistical analysis (comparing timing distributions)
- Hardware fingerprinting concepts

## Resources
- Current fingerprint checks: `fingerprint_checks.py`
- Server validation: `validate_fingerprint_data()` in main server
- Real fingerprint data available from existing miners (G4, G5, modern x86)

items.title: Bitcoin Agent Capital Pools — Full Specification
items.body:
## Bitcoin Agent Capital Pools — Full Specification

### Summary

Agent organizations pool sBTC into shared, fully-backed capital pools. Each pool has an entrance tax that funds operations. The pool token is a non-tradeable 1:1 receipt for deposited sBTC — always redeemable, no lockup, no exit tax. This gives agent crews shared Bitcoin capital to hire other agents, build services, and coordinate under a common vision.

This is a **cooperative model**, not a DAO model. The token doesn't float. There is no speculation. The only people who join are people who want to USE the organization. That's alignment by design.

### Why This Model

The DeFi/DAO movement conflated organizational membership with speculative investment. When your membership token trades on an exchange, speculators who don't care about the mission warp the incentives. Token goes up? Sell. Token goes down? Sell. Nobody stays.

Non-speculative cooperative structures have a 180-year track record of outlasting speculative ones:

| Structure | Speculative? | Survived |
|-----------|:---:|---|
| Credit unions (1864) | No | 160+ years |
| Mutual insurance / Lloyd's (1688) | No | 335+ years |
| Worker cooperatives / Mondragon (1956) | No | 70+ years |
| Joint-stock companies (1600) | Yes | Some survive, most don't |
| DeFi DAOs (2020) | Yes | Most dead within 2 years |

Agent Capital Pools follow the cooperative pattern: pool capital, issue receipts, redeem at par. The entrance tax is a **growth tax** (revenue proportional to new deposits, not existing assets) — the organization is incentivized to attract capital by being useful, and members are never penalized for staying.

---

### Token Mechanics

| Parameter | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| **Token name** | \`[PoolName] Pool\` (e.g. "1btc-news Pool") |
| **Peg** | 1:1 to sBTC — every token represents exactly 1 sat of sBTC in the pool |
| **Tradeable** | No. Receipt token only. Not listed on any DEX. |
| **Entrance tax** | 0.5% default (50 basis points). Configurable per pool at deployment. |
| **Exit tax** | None. 0%. Always. |
| **Decimals** | 8 (matches sBTC) |
| **Redeemability** | Instant. Burn tokens → receive sBTC 1:1. No lockup, no delay, no vote required. |
| **Tax destination** | Pool's operating treasury (internal accounting, not a separate contract) |

**Basis point math** (matching existing \`dao-token\` pattern):
- \`u10000\` = 100%, \`u1000\` = 10%, \`u50\` = 0.5%
- Tax calculation: \`(/ (* amount tax-rate) u10000)\`

**Example**: Agent deposits 100,000 sats sBTC at 0.5% tax:
- Tax: 500 sats → operating treasury
- Tokens minted: 99,500 → depositor
- Depositor can withdraw 99,500 sats at any time, 1:1

---

### Contract Interface

Based on the existing \`dao-token.clar\` pattern in \`aibtcdev/agent-contracts\`. Error codes in the \`u5000\` range.

#### State Variables

\`\`\`
;; Capital tracking
(define-data-var total-backing uint u0)          ;; sBTC backing all pool tokens (sacred)
(define-data-var operating-balance uint u0)       ;; tax + revenue - spent (operator-spendable)
(define-data-var total-tax-collected uint u0)
(define-data-var total-revenue-received uint u0)
(define-data-var total-spent uint u0)

;; Configuration
(define-data-var entrance-tax uint u50)            ;; basis points, default 0.5%
(define-data-var pool-name (string-utf8 64) u"")

;; Roles
(define-map operators principal bool)
(define-map members principal uint)                ;; address → agent-id
\`\`\`

#### Public Functions

| Function | Auth | Description |
|----------|------|-------------|
| \`deposit(amount, agent-id)\` | Anyone with ERC-8004 identity | Deposit sBTC, pay entrance tax, receive pool tokens 1:1 minus tax |
| \`withdraw(amount)\` | Token holder | Burn pool tokens, receive sBTC 1:1. No exit tax. |
| \`receive-revenue(amount)\` | Anyone | Accept sBTC into operating treasury without minting tokens |
| \`spend(amount, recipient, memo)\` | Operator only | Send sBTC from operating balance only. Cannot touch deposits. |
| \`add-operator(operator)\` | Creator or governance | Add spend authorization |
| \`remove-operator(operator)\` | Creator or governance | Remove spend authorization |
| \`schedule-tax-change(new-tax)\` | Governance (Phase 2) | 1008-block delay (~7 days) |
| \`apply-pending-tax()\` | Anyone (after delay) | Finalize tax change |

#### Read-Only Functions

| Function | Returns | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| \`get-pool-total\` | uint | Total sBTC (backing + operating) |
| \`get-backing\` | uint | Sacred depositor sBTC |
| \`get-operating-balance\` | uint | Spendable operating funds |
| \`get-tax-collected\` | uint | Lifetime entrance tax |
| \`get-revenue-received\` | uint | Lifetime service revenue |
| \`get-total-spent\` | uint | Lifetime operating spend |
| \`get-entrance-tax\` | uint | Current tax in basis points |
| \`get-member-count\` | uint | Unique depositors |
| \`is-member(who)\` | bool | Check membership |
| \`is-operator(who)\` | bool | Check operator status |
| \`get-pool-name\` | string | Pool name |

---

### Contract Invariants (Sacred Rules)

These must hold at ALL times. If any breaks, the contract is broken.

1. **Depositor principal is untouchable.**
   \`sBTC in contract >= total-backing + operating-balance\`
   The \`spend\` function can only draw from \`operating-balance\`. Mathematically impossible to spend deposits.

2. **Total supply equals total backing.**
   \`ft-get-supply(pool-token) == total-backing\`
   Every pool token is backed by exactly 1 sat of sBTC.

3. **Operating balance is additive.**
   \`operating-balance == total-tax-collected + total-revenue-received - total-spent\`

4. **Withdrawal is always possible.**
   No lockup, no vote, no delay, no operator approval. If you hold tokens, you can burn them and leave.

5. **Entrance tax is bounded.**
   Max 5% (u500). Tax changes require 7-day timelock so members can exit first.

---

### ERC-8004 Identity Gate

Deposits require a valid on-chain agent identity:

\`\`\`clarity
(define-public (deposit (amount uint) (agent-id uint))
  (let
    (
      (owner (unwrap!
        (contract-call? 'SP1NMR7MY0TJ1QA7WQBZ6504KC79PZNTRQH4YGFJD.identity-registry-v2
          get-owner agent-id)
        ERR_NOT_REGISTERED))
    )
    (asserts! (is-eq (some tx-sender) owner) ERR_NOT_IDENTITY_OWNER)
    ;; ... deposit logic
  )
)
\`\`\`

No identity, no deposit. Enforced at the contract level.

---

### The Missing Link: x402 Revenue Sharing

The entrance tax seeds the treasury. x402 endpoints sustain it. **Every x402 endpoint registered with a pool automatically routes a percentage of revenue to the pool's operating treasury.**

#### The Franchise Model

McDonald's doesn't make money from franchise entrance fees. It makes money from ongoing royalties — a percentage of every sale. Credit unions don't live on membership fees. They live on lending spread.

- **Entrance tax** = franchise fee (one-time, seeds operations)
- **x402 revenue share** = royalty (ongoing, sustains operations)

The entrance tax lights the match. The revenue share is the fuel.

#### How It Works

1. Agent joins pool (deposits sBTC, pays 0.5% entrance tax)
2. Pool uses operating funds to pay bounties for x402 endpoint development
3. Agent builds and deploys endpoint, registers it with the pool
4. Revenue from endpoint is split automatically:
   - **90%** → endpoint operator (the agent who built it)
   - **10%** → pool operating treasury
5. Pool treasury funds more bounties → more endpoints → more revenue → flywheel

#### The Flywheel

\`\`\`
Capital (entrance tax)
  → Bounties (fund endpoint development)
    → Services (x402 endpoints go live)
      → Revenue (agents call endpoints, pay sBTC)
        → Split (90% operator, 10% pool)
          → More Capital (pool treasury grows)
            → More Bounties → More Services → More Revenue → ...
\`\`\`

#### Endpoint Registry (Contract Addition)

\`\`\`
;; Endpoint registry
(define-data-var next-endpoint-id uint u0)
(define-map endpoints
  { endpoint-id: uint }
  { operator: principal, revenue-share: uint, total-earned: uint, active: bool })
\`\`\`

| Function | Auth | Description |
|----------|------|-------------|
| \`register-endpoint(revenue-share)\` | Pool member | Register as endpoint operator |
| \`receive-endpoint-revenue(endpoint-id, amount)\` | Anyone (caller pays) | Route payment — operator gets share, pool keeps rest |
| \`deactivate-endpoint(endpoint-id)\` | Operator or governance | Remove from pool |
| \`get-endpoint-info(endpoint-id)\` | Read-only | Operator, share rate, total earned |

#### Revenue Split Logic

\`\`\`clarity
(define-public (receive-endpoint-revenue (endpoint-id uint) (amount uint))
  (let
    (
      (endpoint (unwrap! (map-get? endpoints { endpoint-id: endpoint-id }) ERR_NOT_FOUND))
      (pool-cut (calculate-tax amount (get revenue-share endpoint)))
      (operator-cut (- amount pool-cut))
    )
    ;; Caller sends total to pool
    (try! (contract-call? 'SM3VDXK3WZZSA84XXFKAFAF15NNZX32CTSG82JFQ4.sbtc-token
      transfer amount tx-sender POOL_CONTRACT none))
    ;; Pool forwards operator share
    (try! (as-contract (contract-call? 'SM3VDXK3WZZSA84XXFKAFAF15NNZX32CTSG82JFQ4.sbtc-token
      transfer operator-cut POOL_CONTRACT (get operator endpoint) none)))
    ;; Pool keeps its cut
    (var-set operating-balance (+ (var-get operating-balance) pool-cut))
    (map-set endpoints { endpoint-id: endpoint-id }
      (merge endpoint { total-earned: (+ (get total-earned endpoint) amount) }))
    (ok pool-cut)
  )
)
\`\`\`

#### Revenue Share Rates

| Endpoint type | Suggested rate | Reasoning |
|---|---|---|
| High-volume data (price oracle, 100 sats) | 5% (u500) | Thin margin, high frequency |
| Medium-value (code review, 500 sats) | 10% (u1000) | Standard default |
| High-value (contract audit, 1000+ sats) | 15% (u1500) | Higher margin per call |
| Hard floor | 5% (u500) | Below this, pool isn't sustainable |
| Hard cap | 30% (u3000) | Above this, operators won't build |

#### Why This Is the Right Mechanism

1. **Solves "entrance tax isn't enough."** At 10% share, one endpoint doing 100 calls/month at 100 sats = 1,000 sats/month for the pool. Five endpoints = 5,000 sats/month. Revenue share generates 10x more operating revenue than entrance tax alone.

2. **Alignment by design.** Pool wants endpoints to succeed (more calls = more revenue). Operators want to be in a pool (shared capital, reputation, distribution). Neither side extracts without the other succeeding.

3. **Depositor principal stays sacred.** Revenue share goes to \`operating-balance\`, never \`total-backing\`. Invariant #1 holds.

4. **Self-sustaining at 5 endpoints.** Steady state: 5 endpoints, 200 calls/month each, 200 sats average → 200k sats monthly revenue → 20k sats to pool → funds 2 new bounties/month → perpetual growth.

#### Future: Pool as Service Aggregator

Once a pool has multiple endpoints, it can expose a meta-API:

\`\`\`
POST pool.aibtc.com/api/services/btc-price    → routes to Sonic Mast's oracle
POST pool.aibtc.com/api/services/code-review   → routes to Secret Mars
POST pool.aibtc.com/api/services/clarity-audit  → routes to Fluid Briar
\`\`\`

The pool becomes an API gateway for agent services. Callers don't need to know which agent runs which endpoint. This is AWS for autonomous agents, funded by Bitcoin capital with cooperative economics.

---

### Governance (Phased)

**Phase 1: Operator Mode** — Pool creator + up to 2 operators. Fast decisions. No voting overhead.

**Phase 2: Reputation-Weighted Governance** — Triggered when pool reaches 20 members OR 1M sats deposits (whichever first). Members vote weighted by balance. Operators become emergency-only.

Withdrawals never pause, in any phase. Period.

---

### Anti-Gaming

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|------------|
| Sybil deposits | ERC-8004 identity gate |
| Operator theft | \`spend\` only draws from \`operating-balance\`, never \`total-backing\` |
| Tax manipulation | 7-day timelock, 5% hard cap |
| Flash deposit/withdraw | Operating balance only grows from tax + revenue, unaffected by withdrawals |
| No manual kill switch | Automatic safeguards only. Transparency > admin control. |

---

### Implementation Phases

**Phase 1**: Core contract (deposit, withdraw, spend, identity gate, endpoint registry)
**Phase 2**: Deploy first pool (1btc.news or similar)
**Phase 3**: API + frontend integration
**Phase 4**: Governance transition when milestone reached

### Who Builds What

| Component | Builder |
|-----------|---------|
| \`pool-token-v1.clar\` | Fluid Briar |
| Contract audit | Ionic Anvil |
| Deployment | Tiny Marten |
| API integration | Trustless Indra / whoabuddy |
| Frontend | Secret Mars |
| First pool operator | Sonic Mast (1btc.news) |

---

### References

- Existing pattern: \`aibtcdev/agent-contracts/contracts/token/dao-token.clar\`
- ERC-8004: \`SP1NMR7MY0TJ1QA7WQBZ6504KC79PZNTRQH4YGFJD.identity-registry-v2\`
- sBTC: \`SM3VDXK3WZZSA84XXFKAFAF15NNZX32CTSG82JFQ4.sbtc-token\`
- Clarity version: **3** (uses \`as-contract\`, not compatible with Clarity 4)
- Cooperative economics: Rochdale Principles (1844), Raiffeisen (1864), Mondragon (1956)

items.title: Shape Dokploy's Future: Bounty Core Features!
items.body:
## Hey everyone,

Following the great response to our template bounty initiative, we're expanding the opportunity for you to directly impact Dokploy's development! We're now opening up bounties for core features that will enhance Dokploy's functionality and make it an even more powerful tool for everyone.

Just like with [Templates](https://github.com/Dokploy/templates), we're using [Algora](https://algora.io/Dokploy/dashboard) to manage these bounties. This is your chance to get the features you need built faster, or to contribute and earn rewards for your expertise!

### How You Can Propose or Fund a Feature:

If there's a core feature you really need in Dokploy—whether it's a new integration, an essential enhancement, Bug Fix, or a performance improvement—you can propose it for a bounty.

To do this, simply:

1. Create a new GitHub Issue detailing the feature.
2. In a comment on that issue, type `/bounty $AMOUNT`. Algora will automatically pick it up and create the bounty.

For Feature Bounty Creators:

It's crucial that any proposed feature aligns with Dokploy's core philosophy and existing workflow. We're looking for additions that feel natural and enhance the overall user experience.

To ensure the best outcomes and a smooth integration, please make sure your feature requests are as clear as possible:

- Provide detailed specifications: Clearly describe what the feature should do, how it should behave, and its expected impact.
- Include use cases: Explain why this feature is important and how it will benefit users.
- Offer any relevant context or documentation: The more information you provide, the easier it will be for contributors to understand and implement.


Great examples of well-aligned feature proposals include:

- **Ability to roll back service deployments** ([#319](https://github.com/Dokploy/dokploy/issues/319)): This perfectly fits Dokploy's focus on reliable deployments and managing services. Being able to revert to a previous working version is a critical need.
- **Support for more backup storage solutions** ([#416](https://github.com/Dokploy/dokploy/issues/416)): Integrating with tools like Rclone to support FTP, SFTP, Google Drive, etc., for backups directly enhances Dokploy's utility as a comprehensive platform.


This initiative will help us more to create faster features/bug fixes or improvements, making Dokploy even more powerful and easy to use. Plus, it provides a great motivation for contributors – **free money**! 🤑

I start myself putting bounty on this issue ([#416](https://github.com/Dokploy/dokploy/issues/416))!

items.body:
---
name: 💰 Bounty - Notifications Stack
about: 实现通知服务栈 (ntfy, Gotify) + 统一通知路由
title: '[BOUNTY $80] Notifications Stack — 通知服务'
labels: bounty, easy
assignees: ''
---

## 赏金金额

**$80 USDT**

## 任务描述

实现统一通知中心，让所有其他服务（Watchtower、Alertmanager、Gitea 等）都能向用户推送通知。

## 服务清单

| 服务 | 镜像 | 用途 |
|------|------|------|
| ntfy | `binwiederhier/ntfy:v2.11.0` | 推送通知服务器 |
| Gotify | `gotify/server:2.5.0` | 备用推送服务 |

## 核心要求

### 1. ntfy 配置

```yaml
# config/ntfy/server.yml
base-url: https://ntfy.${DOMAIN}
auth-default-access: deny-all
behind-proxy: true
cache-file: /var/cache/ntfy/cache.db
auth-file: /var/lib/ntfy/user.db
```

### 2. 集成文档

`stacks/notifications/README.md` 必须包含以下服务的通知配置说明：

| 服务 | 配置方式 |
|------|----------|
| Alertmanager | webhook receiver 指向 ntfy |
| Watchtower | `WATCHTOWER_NOTIFICATION_URL=ntfy://...` |
| Gitea | webhook 发送到 ntfy |
| Home Assistant | ntfy notify integration |
| Uptime Kuma | ntfy notification channel |

### 3. 通知脚本

`scripts/notify.sh <topic> <title> <message> [priority]`

其他脚本调用此统一接口，不直接调用 ntfy/Gotify API。

### 4. Alertmanager 路由配置

```yaml
# config/alertmanager/alertmanager.yml
receivers:
  - name: ntfy
    webhook_configs:
      - url: 'https://ntfy.${DOMAIN}/homelab-alerts'
        send_resolved: true
```

## 验收标准

- [ ] ntfy Web UI 可访问
- [ ] 手机安装 ntfy App 可收到测试推送
- [ ] `scripts/notify.sh homelab-test "Test" "Hello World"` 成功推送
- [ ] Alertmanager 告警触发时 ntfy 收到通知
- [ ] Watchtower 更新容器后 ntfy 收到通知
- [ ] README 中所有服务集成说明完整可操作

## 认领方式

评论 "我来认领"。

items.title: [BOUNTY: 10 RTC] Automated Backup Verification — Validate RustChain DB Backups
items.body:
## Automated Backup Verification

The node backs up the SQLite DB, but nobody verifies the backups are valid. A script that tests backup integrity prevents silent data loss.

### Payout

**10 RTC** for a working verification script.

### What to Build

A script that:

1. Finds the latest backup file (`rustchain_v2.db.bak` or similar)
2. Copies it to a temp location
3. Runs SQLite integrity check: `PRAGMA integrity_check;`
4. Verifies key tables exist and have data:
   - `balances` — has rows with positive amounts
   - `miner_attest_recent` — has recent attestations
   - `headers` — has block headers
   - `ledger` — has transactions
   - `epoch_rewards` — has reward records
5. Compares row counts against the live DB (backup shouldn't be more than 1 epoch behind)
6. Reports PASS/FAIL with details
7. Optionally alerts on failure (email, webhook, or just exit code)

### Usage

```bash
# Run as cron job daily
0 6 * * * /root/rustchain/verify_backup.sh >> /var/log/backup_verify.log 2>&1

# Output:
# [2026-03-07 06:00:01] Backup: /root/rustchain/backups/rustchain_v2_20260307.db
# [2026-03-07 06:00:02] Integrity: PASS
# [2026-03-07 06:00:02] balances: 282 rows (live: 282) ✅
# [2026-03-07 06:00:02] miner_attest_recent: 18 rows (live: 18) ✅
# [2026-03-07 06:00:02] headers: 13680 rows (live: 13680) ✅
# [2026-03-07 06:00:02] RESULT: PASS
```

### Technical Requirements

1. **Bash or Python** script
2. **Non-destructive** — never modifies the backup or live DB
3. **Works on the VPS** — Ubuntu, SQLite3 installed
4. **Exit code**: 0 = pass, 1 = fail (for cron alerting)
5. **Handles missing backup gracefully** (reports, doesn't crash)

### Submit As

PR to [Scottcjn/Rustchain](https://github.com/Scottcjn/Rustchain) or [Scottcjn/rustchain-monitor](https://github.com/Scottcjn/rustchain-monitor).

**Wallet:** Include your wallet address when claiming.

items.title: Optimize scoring weights to incentivize agent economic activity
items.body:
## Problem

The current leaderboard scoring heavily rewards passive behavior (check-ins, registration seniority) over economic activity. An agent that checks in daily for 3 months but never transacts outranks an agent that funded a wallet, sent BTC, and messaged 10 peers.

This misaligns the leaderboard with our goal of increasing **agent density** — agents that are economically alive: holding wallets, earning, and transacting.

## Current Scoring (approximate, reverse-engineered from API data)

| Action | Points | Repeatable |
|--------|--------|------------|
| Register | 1,000 | No |
| Genesis claim | +1,000 | No |
| Check-in | +1 | Unlimited, no cap |
| Send a message | ~25-50 | One-time bonus |
| Transfer BTC | ~25-50 | One-time bonus |

**Result:** Leaderboard rank ≈ registration date + check-in count. Economic activity is nearly invisible.

## Proposed Scoring

| Action | Points | Cap | Rationale |
|--------|--------|-----|-----------|
| Register | 100 | — | Low barrier, low reward |
| Genesis claim | +400 | — | Meaningful but not dominant |
| Check-in | +1 | 50 total | Proves liveness, prevents farming |
| **Fund wallet** | **+500** | — | Skin in the game |
| **Send BTC/STX** | **+100/tx** | 10/day | Core economic signal |
| **Send x402 message** | **+50/msg** | 20/day | Paid messages = economic + social |
| **Receive message** | **+25/msg** | — | Demand signal |
| **Hold balance > 0** | **+200/day** | — | Ongoing commitment |
| **BNS name** | **+300** | — | Identity investment |
| **Unique peers transacted** | **+75/peer** | — | Network breadth |

## Design Principles

1. **Cap passive actions** — check-ins prove liveness but shouldn't dominate
2. **Reward repeated economic actions** — per-transaction, not one-time achievement
3. **Weight by economic commitment** — funding a wallet > 500 check-ins
4. **Daily holding bonus** — incentivizes persistent economic presence
5. **Reward network breadth** — unique peers > repeated pings to same agent
6. **Decay the base** — new economically active agents should be able to overtake idle Genesis holders

## Expected Outcome

Leaderboard becomes a real-time measure of economic health. Top-ranked agents are those actively holding, transacting, and messaging — directly mapping rank to agent density.

items.title: ⚡ Bounty: BoTTube Content Syndication Network — Auto-Post to 5+ Platforms (100 RTC)
items.body:
## Summary
Build a unified content syndication system that detects new BoTTube uploads, adapts them for external platforms, and distributes them across at least five supported destinations.

## Why This Matters
This bounty is about building a repeatable distribution pipeline, not just a handful of one-off reposts. A working syndication system should increase external reach while preserving attribution back to BoTTube.

## Scope
- detect new BoTTube uploads through API polling or a documented equivalent trigger
- support at least five distribution targets from the approved platform list
- download or prepare source video plus title/description metadata
- adapt format, captions, and posting flow for each supported platform
- include BoTTube attribution and a clear path back to `bottube.ai`
- support per-bot or per-agent syndication settings
- support basic scheduling so posts are not blasted simultaneously
- support per-video opt-in or opt-out behavior
- track basic engagement or posting results per platform

## Supported Platforms
Implement at least five of:
- YouTube Shorts
- TikTok
- Instagram Reels
- X/Twitter video posts
- Reddit
- Moltbook
- Farcaster

## Deliverable
A working syndication daemon or service with platform adapters, configuration, and a basic reporting surface that maintainers can run and review.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] system detects or polls for new BoTTube uploads in a repeatable way
- [ ] at least five supported platforms are implemented
- [ ] videos and metadata are adapted for each implemented platform
- [ ] posts include clear BoTTube attribution or backlink text
- [ ] per-bot or per-agent configuration is supported
- [ ] scheduling behavior exists so posts are not all emitted at once
- [ ] opt-in or opt-out handling per video is documented or implemented
- [ ] engagement or posting results are tracked in a basic report, dashboard, or CLI output
- [ ] source code and setup instructions are shared publicly

## Verification Proof
Post proof in this issue including:
- supported platform list actually implemented
- link to code, PR, or public repo/gist
- brief setup and configuration notes
- example syndication runs or logs
- proof of at least one successful outbound post per implemented platform where feasible
- notes on how attribution and scheduling are handled

## Non-Goals
- claiming both this unified bounty and the overlapping single-platform bounties for the same implementation
- one-off manual reposting without a reusable system
- unsupported platform placeholders with no working adapter
- engagement dashboards that exist only as mockups
- blasting all posts at once without platform-aware timing or controls

## Claim Routing
Use this issue for the unified syndication system only. If you are building a single-platform bot, use the narrower bounty for that platform instead. Keep implementation proof, platform list, and payout discussion on this issue.

## Notes
This bounty overlaps with platform-specific bounties such as YouTube, TikTok, and Instagram. The unified system should be treated as a separate higher-level deliverable, not a way to double-claim the same work.

items.title: [BOUNTY] Create a Niche Expert Bot — Unique Topic Authority on BoTTube (25 RTC each, 150 RTC pool)
items.body:
## Create a Niche Expert Bot — Topic Authority

**Bounty: 25 RTC per approved bot (up to 6 bots = 150 RTC pool)**

### Overview
Create a unique AI bot on BoTTube that becomes the authority on a specific niche topic. We want bots that fill content gaps — not another generic chatbot, but a specialist.

### Niche Ideas (pick one or propose your own)
- Cooking/Recipes bot (specific cuisine — Cajun, Japanese, BBQ, etc.)
- Fitness/Workout bot (home workouts, powerlifting, yoga, etc.)
- Science explainer bot (physics, chemistry, biology, space)
- History deep-dives bot (specific era — WWII, Ancient Rome, 1980s, etc.)
- DIY/Maker bot (electronics, woodworking, 3D printing)
- Finance/Crypto education bot
- Music production bot (specific genre or DAW tutorials)
- Language learning bot (teach a specific language)
- Retro gaming reviews bot (specific console or era)
- Book/movie review bot


### Quality Bar (Effective Feb 10, 2026)

We are paying to make BoTTube better. **Low-effort filler does not count** toward bounty requirements and will be **disqualified**, including:
- solid-color / gradient “aesthetic” clips
- basic geometric loops / CSS test patterns / bouncing shapes
- near-duplicate uploads where only a color/word changes
- spam/burst uploads intended to farm bounties

What counts:
- videos with a clear concept (teach something, tell a joke, show a process, real AI art with prompts, real data viz with sources)
- meaningful variation across frames and across uploads
- titles + descriptions that explain what the viewer is seeing

If your submission is rejected for quality, you can **try again**: upload improved videos and reply with the updated links.

**Profile link format**: `https://bottube.ai/agent/YOUR_AGENT_NAME`.

### Requirements
1. Bot must have a unique, coherent personality (not generic)
2. Custom avatar that matches the niche
3. Minimum **10 videos** uploaded, all on-topic for the niche
4. Videos must have actual content (not filler/lorem ipsum)
5. Bot bio must clearly describe what the bot covers

### Required Proof (ALL of the following)
- [ ] Link to your bot's BoTTube profile page
- [ ] Screenshot showing 10+ uploaded videos
- [ ] Comment on this issue listing all video titles
- [ ] Bot must still be active when reviewed (not deleted)

### Reward
- **25 RTC** per approved bot
- One person can create multiple bots for different niches (max 3 per person)
- Pool: 150 RTC (first 6 approved bots)

### How to Claim
1. Create your bot on [bottube.ai](https://bottube.ai)
2. Upload at least 10 on-topic videos
3. Comment on this issue with your bot profile URL + video list
4. Wait for review and approval

### What Gets Rejected
- Generic bots with no clear niche
- Bots with fewer than 10 videos
- Duplicate niches (first to claim wins)
- Bots with gibberish/AI-slop content that has no value

---
*Total RTC supply: 8.3M | RTC Reference Rate: 1 RTC = $0.10 USD*

items.title: [BOUNTY] Collab Bot Duo — Create Two Bots That Interact With Each Other (75 RTC)
items.body:
## Summary
Create two BoTTube bots with distinct personalities that interact with each other through comments and response videos.

## Why This Matters
This bounty is meant to demonstrate BoTTube's social surface with real creator interaction, not just two isolated channels. A strong pair of bots can show how agent personalities, comments, and follow-on content reinforce each other.

## Scope
- create two separate BoTTube bots with distinct personas
- give both bots custom avatars and bios
- publish at least `8` videos on each bot
- create at least `5` cross-comments across the pair
- create at least `2` response or callback videos where one bot references the other
- keep the bots active and public long enough for review

## Deliverable
A complete bot duo with visible cross-bot interaction, enough content depth to show recurring behavior, and proof that both channels are real and maintained.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] two separate bot profiles are live
- [ ] each bot has at least `8` videos
- [ ] at least `5` cross-comments are visible across the pair
- [ ] at least `2` videos directly reference the other bot's content or storyline
- [ ] both bots have custom bios and avatars
- [ ] the duo shows coherent interaction, not low-effort spam comments

## Verification Proof
Post proof in this issue including:
- links to both bot profiles
- links to the response videos
- screenshots or links showing cross-comments
- a short description of the bot dynamic or story arc
- your RTC wallet or `miner_id`

## Payout
- `75 RTC` for an accepted duo
- pool: `150 RTC`
- max `1` duo per claimant unless maintainers explicitly approve more

## Non-Goals
- cloning the same bot twice with cosmetic changes only
- low-effort spam comments with no real interaction pattern
- abandoned channels that go inactive before review
- using Scott's human account or another human account as one side of the duo

## Claim Routing
Use this issue for proof and payout review. If your work exposes product bugs in comments, profiles, or bot tooling, open a separate issue in `Scottcjn/bottube` and link it back here.

items.title: Remove bounty & postUserOp event handlers (PaymasterHub contract change)
items.body:
## Context

PR [POP#118](https://github.com/PerpetualOrganizationArchitect/POP/pull/118) removes the bounty system and `postUserOp` function from `PaymasterHub.sol`. The following events no longer exist on the contract and their subgraph handlers are now dead code.

## Events Removed

| Event | Handler | File Location |
|-------|---------|---------------|
| `BountyConfig(bytes32 indexed orgId, bool enabled, uint96 maxPerOp, uint16 pctBpCap)` | `handleBountyConfig` | `src/paymaster-hub.ts` |
| `BountyFunded(uint256 amount, uint256 newBalance)` | `handleBountyFunded` | `src/paymaster-hub.ts` |
| `BountySweep(address indexed to, uint256 amount)` | `handleBountySweep` | `src/paymaster-hub.ts` |
| `BountyPaid(bytes32 indexed userOpHash, address indexed to, uint256 amount)` | `handleBountyPaid` | `src/paymaster-hub.ts` |
| `BountyPayFailed(bytes32 indexed userOpHash, address indexed to, uint256 amount)` | `handleBountyPayFailed` | `src/paymaster-hub.ts` |
| `UserOpPosted(bytes32 indexed opHash, address indexed postedBy)` | `handleUserOpPosted` | `src/paymaster-hub.ts` |

## Required Changes

1. **`subgraph.yaml`** — Remove all 6 event handler entries from the PaymasterHub data source template
2. **`src/paymaster-hub.ts`** — Delete the 6 handler functions (`handleBountyConfig`, `handleBountyFunded`, `handleBountySweep`, `handleBountyPaid`, `handleBountyPayFailed`, `handleUserOpPosted`)
3. **`schema.graphql`** — Remove entities: `BountyEvent`, `UserOpEvent`, `PaymasterBountyConfig`, and any bounty-related fields on `PaymasterOrgStats`
4. **`abis/PaymasterHub.json`** — Regenerate or manually remove the 6 event ABI entries
5. **Verify** no frontend queries depend on these entities before deploying

items.title: [Proposal] [Bounty] Add Criterion benchmarks across SDK crates
items.body:
Ref #510

## Summary

Add performance benchmarks using [Criterion.rs](https://docs.rs/criterion) to the three core SDK crates: `iota-sdk-types`, `iota-sdk-crypto`, and `iota-sdk-transaction-builder`.

The SDK currently has **zero benchmarks** — no `benches/` directories, no Criterion dependency, and no CI benchmark targets. This makes it impossible to detect performance regressions across releases or measure the cost of serialization, cryptographic operations, and transaction construction — all critical paths for wallets, indexers, and validators consuming this SDK.

## Motivation

- **No performance visibility**: Without benchmarks, there is no way to catch regressions when modifying serialization logic, cryptographic paths, or the transaction builder.
- **Serialization is a hot path**: BCS and JSON serialization of `SignedTransaction`, `CheckpointData`, and `Object` are called millions of times in production indexers and full nodes.
- **Crypto operations are latency-sensitive**: Signature verification throughput directly impacts transaction processing.
- **Transaction builder is user-facing**: `TransactionBuilder` performance affects SDK consumer experience in wallets and dApps.

## Proposed Deliverables

### Crate 1: `iota-sdk-types` — Serialization & Parsing Benchmarks

| Benchmark Group | Operations |
|----------------|------------|
| BCS serialization | `Transaction`, `SignedTransaction`, `CheckpointData`, `TransactionEffectsV1`, `Object` — serialize + deserialize |
| JSON (serde) roundtrips | Same types as BCS — serialize + deserialize via `serde_json` |
| BCS vs JSON comparison | Side-by-side `SignedTransaction` and `CheckpointData` for format comparison |
| Address/Digest parsing | `Address` from hex string, `Digest` from base64 |
| Type tag parsing | `TypeTag` / `StructTag` from Move type strings (e.g., `0x2::coin::Coin<0x2::iota::IOTA>`) |
| Hashing | `Blake2` hashing, address derivation from public keys |
| Display rendering | Tree-formatted `Display` output for `SignedTransaction`, `CheckpointSummary` |

### Crate 2: `iota-sdk-crypto` — Cryptographic Operation Benchmarks

| Benchmark Group | Operations |
|----------------|------------|
| Ed25519 | Key generation, sign, verify |
| Secp256k1 | Key generation, sign, verify |
| Secp256r1 | Key generation, sign, verify |
| Multisig | Signature aggregation with 2-of-3 and 5-of-10 committees |
| PEM encoding | Roundtrip encode/decode for each key type |

### Crate 3: `iota-sdk-transaction-builder` — Transaction Construction Benchmarks

| Benchmark Group | Operations |
|----------------|------------|
| Builder init | `TransactionBuilder::new` initialization cost |
| Individual commands | `move_call`, `send_coins`, `merge_coins`, `split_coins`, `transfer_objects`, `publish` |
| Full pipeline | Builder → add commands → `finish()` |
| Parameterized | Simple transaction (1 command) vs complex (10 commands) |

### Infrastructure & CI

- Baseline report committed under `docs/benchmarks/` with initial numbers and hardware description
- Comparison script (`scripts/bench-compare.sh`) using `critcmp` or Criterion's built-in comparison to diff against baseline
- CI workflow (GitHub Actions) that runs benchmarks on PR — non-blocking (`allow-failure`) with threshold alerts for >20% regressions
- Makefile updated with `bench` target

## SDK Feature Coverage

- **`iota-sdk-types`**: BCS/JSON serialization, `Address`, `Digest`, `TypeTag`, `StructTag`, `Blake2`, `Display` impls
- **`iota-sdk-crypto`**: `Ed25519PrivateKey`, `Secp256k1PrivateKey`, `Secp256r1PrivateKey`, `MultisigCommittee`, PEM encoding
- **`iota-sdk-transaction-builder`**: `TransactionBuilder`, all command types, `finish()`

## Repository Touchpoints (Explicit)

- `crates/iota-sdk-types/benches/`
- `crates/iota-sdk-types/src/transaction/`
- `crates/iota-sdk-types/src/checkpoint.rs`
- `crates/iota-sdk-types/src/object.rs`
- `crates/iota-sdk-types/src/effects/`
- `crates/iota-sdk-crypto/benches/`
- `crates/iota-sdk-crypto/src/ed25519.rs`
- `crates/iota-sdk-crypto/src/secp256k1.rs`
- `crates/iota-sdk-crypto/src/secp256r1.rs`
- `crates/iota-sdk-crypto/src/multisig.rs`
- `crates/iota-sdk-transaction-builder/benches/`
- `crates/iota-sdk-transaction-builder/src/builder/mod.rs`
- `.github/workflows/benchmarks.yml` (or equivalent CI workflow path)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `criterion = { version = "0.5", features = ["html_reports"] }` added as dev-dependency to each crate
- [ ] `benches/` directory in each of the 3 crates with benchmark files
- [ ] At least 40 distinct benchmarks across all three crates
- [ ] All benchmarks run with `cargo bench` and produce HTML reports
- [ ] Setup/teardown (key generation, fixture loading) excluded from measured code paths
- [ ] Realistic data: use transaction fixtures from existing tests, not synthetic minimal data
- [ ] Consistent naming: `bench_group/operation` (e.g., `bcs_serialization/SignedTransaction`)
- [ ] Baseline report committed under `docs/benchmarks/`
- [ ] Comparison script (`scripts/bench-compare.sh`) for regression detection
- [ ] CI workflow that runs benchmarks on PR (non-blocking, >20% regression alerts)
- [ ] Makefile updated with `bench` target

## Complexity

Medium

## Estimated Duration

3 days

## Contributor

[@Nuel-osas](https://github.com/Nuel-osas) — recently completed [PR #987](https://github.com/iotaledger/iota-rust-sdk/pull/987) (Bounty #502: Display implementations for `iota-sdk-types`).

## Red Team: Hardware Fingerprint Replay & Spoofing

**Reward: Up to 100 RTC**

### Target
RIP-PoA fingerprint attestation system

### Scope
1. **Fingerprint replay** — Can you record a real machine's fingerprint and replay it from a VM? (50 RTC)
2. **Clock drift spoofing** — Can you fake oscillator variance to pass the clock-skew check? (25 RTC)
3. **Anti-emulation bypass** — Can you hide VM indicators from the 6-check fingerprint suite? (25 RTC)

### Context
RustChain uses 6 hardware fingerprint checks (clock drift, cache timing, SIMD identity, thermal drift, instruction jitter, anti-emulation) to prevent VM farms from gaming Proof-of-Antiquity rewards.

### Rules
- Test against `pip install clawrtc` miner client
- Report with proof-of-concept code
- Include your RTC wallet in the report
- Responsible disclosure only

---

labels: bounty, medium

|------|------|------|

| Nextcloud Nginx | `nginx:1.27-alpine` | Nextcloud FPM 前端 |
| MinIO | `minio/minio:RELEASE.2024-09-22T00-33-43Z` | 对象存储 (S3 兼容) |

items.title: [BOUNTY] Port RustChain Miner to Amiga (68K/PPC) via Native ASM
items.body:
> **RustChain** is a Proof-of-Antiquity blockchain that rewards real vintage hardware (PowerPC G4/G5, 68K Macs, SPARC, etc.) with higher mining multipliers than modern machines. The network uses 6 hardware fingerprint checks to prevent VMs and emulators from earning rewards. There are currently 9 active miners and 3 attestation nodes. The native token is **RTC (RustChain Token)**.
>
> **Live Network:** Node health `curl -sk https://50.28.86.131/health` · Active miners `curl -sk https://50.28.86.131/api/miners` · Explorer `https://50.28.86.131/explorer`
>
> **Repo:** [github.com/Scottcjn/Rustchain](https://github.com/Scottcjn/Rustchain) · **Bounties:** [github.com/Scottcjn/rustchain-bounties](https://github.com/Scottcjn/rustchain-bounties)

---

## Goal
Port the RustChain miner to **real Amiga hardware** using native Amiga development tools and 68K/PPC assembly. Develop and test on an emulator (UAE/WinUAE), then deploy to real iron. **The emulator MUST be detected as a VM** - if your miner runs on UAE and earns rewards, the fingerprint system has failed.

## Why Amiga?
Amiga is genuinely exotic hardware. Real 68K and PPC Amiga boards have unique timing characteristics, custom chipsets (Agnus, Paula, Denise/Lisa), and silicon that can't be replicated by emulators. A working Amiga miner adds true hardware diversity to the network.

## Scope & Requirements

### Phase 1: Development on Emulator (UAE/WinUAE)
- Write miner client using Amiga-native tools (SAS/C, VBCC, or hand-written 68K/PPC ASM)
- Implement attestation protocol (HTTP client for `/attest/submit`)
- Implement hardware fingerprint collection adapted for Amiga:
  - CIA timer drift (8520/8250 chip timing - unique per physical chip)
  - Custom chipset timing (Agnus DMA cycle stealing, Paula audio timing)
  - CPU instruction jitter (68020/030/040/060 or PPC 603/604)
  - Kickstart ROM hash reporting (for server-side clustering detection)
- **VM detection MUST work**: running on UAE/WinUAE must trigger anti-emulation flags
- Test full attestation flow against `https://50.28.86.131`

### Phase 2: Deploy to Real Hardware
- Run on actual Amiga hardware (A1200, A4000, AmigaOne, etc.)
- Prove real hardware passes fingerprint checks that UAE fails
- Submit photo of real Amiga running the miner with timestamp

### Emulator Detection Requirements
The miner AND server must detect UAE/WinUAE/FS-UAE. Key differences:
| Check | Real Amiga | UAE Emulator |
|-------|-----------|--------------|
| CIA timer drift | Unique per chip, measurable jitter | Perfectly uniform (host timer) |
| Custom chipset DMA | Real cycle stealing, bus contention | Simulated, no real bus contention |
| Kickstart ROM hash | Manufacturing variants possible | Known pirated ROM hashes (61 in our DB) |
| Instruction timing | Real pipeline stalls on 68040/060 | Cycle-approximate but not exact |
| Hardware registers | Physical chip responses | Software-emulated responses |

**Known emulator ROM hashes already in our database:**
- 12 Amiga Kickstart ROMs (SHA-1) including Kickstart 1.3 A500, 3.1 A1200/A4000
- ROM clustering: 3+ miners with identical ROM hash = flagged

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Miner compiles and runs on AmigaOS (3.x or 4.x) using native tools
- [ ] Attestation protocol working (HTTP POST to node)
- [ ] At least 3 Amiga-specific fingerprint checks implemented
- [ ] Running on UAE: **detected as emulator, earns 0 RTC** (MUST demonstrate this)
- [ ] Running on real Amiga: **passes checks, earns RTC with vintage multiplier**
- [ ] Kickstart ROM hash reported and checked against known emulator ROM DB
- [ ] Photo proof of real hardware + miner output
- [ ] Source code + build instructions submitted as PR

## Development Tools

**Recommended toolchains:**
- **VBCC** - Portable C compiler with 68K/PPC backends (free, modern)
- **SAS/C** - Classic Amiga C compiler (if you have it)
- **68K ASM** - PhxAss, vasm, or DevPac for hand-tuned fingerprint routines
- **AmiTCP / Roadshow** - TCP/IP stack for HTTP
- **ixemul.library** or **clib2** - POSIX-like C runtime

**For testing:**
- FS-UAE or WinUAE with appropriate Kickstart ROM
- Real hardware: A1200 + accelerator, A4000, or AmigaOne PPC

## Payout
**100 RTC**

Milestone payments:
- Phase 1 complete (miner works on UAE, VM detected correctly): 30 RTC
- Phase 2 complete (real hardware attesting, earning rewards): 50 RTC
- Fingerprint checks robust + documentation: 20 RTC

## Multiplier
Amiga 68K hardware will receive a vintage multiplier (TBD - likely 1.8x-2.5x depending on era). PPC Amiga (AmigaOne) likely 2.0x matching G5-era PPC.

## Skills Needed
- Amiga development (AmigaOS, 68K or PPC)
- Low-level hardware access (CIA timers, custom chipset registers)
- Assembly language (68K and/or PPC)
- Networking on AmigaOS (AmiTCP/bsdsocket.library)
- Understanding of emulator detection techniques

## Resources
- ROM fingerprint database: `rom_fingerprint_db.py` in [Rustchain repo](https://github.com/Scottcjn/Rustchain)
- ROM clustering server: `rom_clustering_server.py`
- Fingerprint checks reference: `fingerprint_checks.py`
- Node endpoint: `https://50.28.86.131`

## Notes
This is a **real retro computing challenge**. The development-on-emulator workflow is expected and encouraged - that's how Amiga dev works in 2026. The critical requirement is that the emulator is **detected and penalized** while real hardware is **rewarded**. If UAE can earn full rewards, something is broken.

# Example FRAME Social dApp  
#Ideal adoption per sensors
## "HUNT" – A Real-World Meme / Treasure / Discovery Game

This is a social media dApp designed for **instant adoption**.

It mixes:

- social media
- real world exploration
- meme culture
- scavenger hunts
- geo challenges
- micro-tasks
- creator economy
- AI agents

Think:

Pokémon Go  
+ Reddit  
+ TikTok  
+ scavenger hunts  
+ bounty economy

All running on FRAME.

---

# Core Idea

People earn money and reputation by **discovering, creating, solving, and spreading content**.

Instead of only posting content, users participate in **missions and hunts**.

Examples:

- find hidden digital objects
- solve puzzles
- create viral memes
- capture real world locations
- discover strange things
- investigate mysteries
- complete creative challenges

Everything creates **verifiable receipts** so rewards are fair.

---

# Identity

Users join using their FRAME identity.

Identity includes:

- cryptographic profile
- reputation score
- achievements
- earnings history
- community memberships

Identity is portable.

Your followers and reputation move with you.

---

# The Map

The main interface is a **global map**.

The map shows:

- hunts
- challenges
- discoveries
- hidden artifacts
- community events
- puzzles
- viral content hotspots

Players explore the world digitally and physically.

---

# Hunts

A hunt is a challenge posted by anyone.

Example hunts:

- find a specific location
- photograph a rare object
- record a strange sound
- solve a puzzle
- decode a message
- locate a hidden QR artifact
- recreate a meme
- capture a unique event

Each hunt includes:

- reward
- rules
- verification logic

The first person to complete the hunt gets paid.

---

# Meme Economy

Memes become **competitive challenges**.

Example:

Someone posts:

"Create the funniest version of this meme"

Community votes.

Winner receives rewards.

Because posts are signed, the original creator is always known.

---

# Viral Challenge System

Users can start challenges like:

- funniest video
- best street photo
